events.title: US Strikes Marine Control Tower in Iran
events.desc: The United States has launched strikes targeting a marine control tower in Iran, escalating the ongoing conflict. This action is part of a broader wave of strikes aimed at degrading Iranian military capabilities, particularly those used against commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: US Launches New Round of Strikes Against Iran
events.desc: The U.S. has initiated a new series of strikes against Iran, coinciding with the reimposition of a naval blockade on Iranian ports. This escalation is part of the ongoing conflict over control of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy chokepoint.

events.title: US Military Reports Launching More Strikes Against Iran
events.desc: The U.S. military confirmed it is carrying out additional strikes against Iran. These actions come as the U.S. prepares to reinstate a blockade on Iranian shipping, following Iran's assertion of closing the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: US Conducts Wave of Strikes on Iran Amid Return to War
events.desc: The United States has executed a significant wave of strikes against Iran, marking a return to hostilities. These attacks are intended to further degrade Iranian military capabilities that have been used to target commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: US Expands Sanctions Targeting Iran's Oil and Cryptocurrency Sectors
events.desc: The United States has broadened its sanctions regime against Iran, focusing on the nation's oil sector and cryptocurrency activities. This move targets the network of Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani, which is described as a key player in Iran's oil exports and global commodities trading.

events.title: Iran Threatens to Block More Trade Routes as US Launches Fresh Strikes
events.desc: Iran has issued threats to obstruct additional trade routes in response to the latest U.S. strikes. President Donald Trump has also vowed to target Iran's bridges and power plants if negotiations are not resumed.

events.title: Trump Threatens to Bomb Bridges and Power Plants Unless Iran Resumes Talks
events.desc: President Trump has escalated rhetoric by threatening to bomb Iranian infrastructure, including bridges and power plants, unless the country agrees to resume negotiations. This follows several days of exchanges of fire and the U.S. reinstating a blockade of Iranian ports.

events.title: US and Iran Trade Fire as Tensions Rise Over Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The U.S. and Iran have engaged in retaliatory strikes, further intensifying tensions around the Strait of Hormuz. Iran claims to have struck U.S. military assets in Kuwait, Jordan, Bahrain, and Oman in response to U.S. actions.

events.title: US and Iran Trade Attacks Amidst New Regime's Ascension
events.desc: The U.S. and Iran have exchanged fire during a period of significant political transition in Iran, following the burial of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. U.S. Central Command reported striking 90 Iranian targets, with Iran's health ministry stating 14 deaths since Tuesday.

events.title: US Insists Strait of Hormuz is Open Amidst Exchanges with Iran
events.desc: U.S. officials maintain that the Strait of Hormuz remains open for navigation, despite Iran's claims of closure and subsequent attacks on U.S. allies and regional bases. Iran has reportedly informed U.S. officials that a recent attack on ships was an 'accident'.

events.title: Israeli Strike on Gaza Police Post Kills Seven
events.desc: An Israeli airstrike on a police post in northern Gaza has resulted in the deaths of seven individuals, including a senior officer from the Hamas-run police force. The Israeli military stated that the targets were 'terrorists'.

events.title: Yemen's Houthis Launch Missiles at Saudi Arabia After Strikes on Sanaa
events.desc: The Iran-backed Houthi movement has launched missiles targeting Saudi Arabia, specifically Abha airport, in response to what they claim were Saudi attacks on Sanaa airport. This action further escalates the conflict in Yemen.

events.title: G7 Urges Halt to Attacks in Sudan's El-Obeid, Calls for Arms Embargo
events.desc: G7 foreign ministers and the EU's foreign policy chief have called for an immediate cessation of hostilities in El-Obeid, Sudan, urging all parties, including the Sudanese army and Rapid Support Forces, to halt actions that endanger civilians. They also advocated for a broader arms embargo.

events.title: Briton Jailed in Iran Receives Additional Two-Year Sentence
events.desc: The family of Craig Foreman, a British national serving a 10-year sentence in Iran for espionage charges he denies, has reported that his sentence has been extended by two years for speaking to the media. This adds to the existing sentence for him and his wife, Lindsay Foreman.

events.title: Far-right Israeli Lawmaker Raids Palestinian School
events.desc: An Israeli education committee official has raided a Palestinian school in Jerusalem, threatening its closure due to the display of Palestinian symbols. This action highlights ongoing tensions and Israeli policy within occupied East Jerusalem.
